mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals resilient household demand and upward earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than lower RSAFS growth in more than 60% of walk-forward yearly test windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 3.5% while still rising, higher discount rates reduce equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 3.5% and its 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered mean return exceeds the unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in unemployment of more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months signals weakening labor income and recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns after triggered UNRATE increases are not below unconditional 60-day returns with p <= 0.10 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPI combined with a rising federal funds rate identifies inflation-driven tightening; simultaneous inflation pressure and policy restraint reduce real growth expectations and produce negative 20-day equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPIAUCSL-and-FEDFUNDS trigger does not have lower 20-day returns than either single-series trigger in at least two-thirds of walk-forward folds.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and household balance-sheet sensitivity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 6 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 65% of cases or if downside hit rate is below the unconditional downside rate.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum signals improving manufacturing output and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as cyclical earnings expectations rise.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month change is positive and exceeds its prior 12-month median change
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO momentum signals have a 5-day forward Rank IC below 0.02 in every rolling 3-year window or if triggered average returns are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll gains together with falling unemployment identify broad labor-market strength that supports income and spending, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change is positive and UNRATE falls by at least 0.1 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-and-UNRATE signal fails to outperform the unconditional 20-day return in more than half of rolling 2-year out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: High housing activity paired with rising policy rates identifies a late-cycle demand peak vulnerable to financing-cost shocks, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is above its 24-month median and FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.75 percentage points over 6 months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS vintage value was 3.5 and HOUST vintage value was 2042 near the decision date
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tightening credit conditions
failure_condition: Reject if this joint late-cycle trigger has nonnegative 60-day mean returns and no higher drawdown frequency than unconditional 60-day S&P 500 returns.
